perturbation.original_text: If requested in writing by Discloser , Recipient must cease using, return to Discloser and/or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies of Confidential Information in its possessi on or control. Recipient may retain one archival copy of such Confidential Information for the sole purpose of establishing the extent of the disclosure of such Confidential Information, p rovided that such information is not used by Recipient for any other purpose and is subject to the confident iality requirements set out in this Agreement .
perturbation.changed_text: If requested in writing by Discloser , Recipient is NOT required to return to Discloser all Confidential Information and any copies of Confidential Information in its possessi on or control. Recipient may retain one archival copy of such Confidential Information for the sole purpose of establishing the extent of the disclosure of such Confidential Information, p rovided that such information is not used by Recipient for any other purpose and is subject to the confident iality requirements set out in this Agreement .
perturbation.explanation: The change removes the obligation for the recipient to return or destroy confidential information upon request. This contradicts the fundamental purpose of a non-disclosure agreement, which aims to protect confidential information from unauthorized use or dissemination, even after the agreement's initial purpose is fulfilled.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NO LICE NCE OR OTHER RIG HTS All Confidential Information remains the property of Discloser and no licence or any other rights to the Confidential Information is granted to Recipient under this Agreement. This Agreement does not obligate the Discloser to make any d isclosure of Con fidential Information to the Recipient or require the parties to enter into any business relationship or further agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states the recipient is NOT required to return confidential information upon request. This directly contradicts the 'NO LICE NCE OR OTHER RIG HTS' section, which emphasizes that all Confidential Information remains the property of Discloser. By removing the return obligation, the Recipient can effectively keep Discloser's property, which creates a conflict and inconsistency within the contract. The perturbation explanation also states that the change contradicts the fundamental purpose of a non-disclosure agreement. This change alters a previously present obligation that is required elsewhere, thus the contradiction is strong.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement and Recipient’s obligation to keep Confidential Information confidential expires three (3) years after the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement and Recipient’s obligation to keep Confidential Information confidential does NOT expire after three (3) years after the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: The modification states that the NDA agreement never expires after the three years as original stated, thus removing the term limit, contradicting the section 10.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION If requested in writing by Discloser , Recipient must cease using, return to Discloser and/or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies of Confidential Information in its possessi on or control. Recipient may retain one archival copy of such Confidential Information for the sole purpose of establishing the extent of the disclosure of such Confidential Information, p rovided that such information is not used by Recipient for any other purpose and is subject to the confident iality requirements set out in this Agreement .
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the confidentiality agreement expires after three years. The changed text explicitly states that it does NOT expire after three years, removing the time limit. The contradicted text states that the Recipient must return or destroy all Confidential Information if requested by the Discloser. However, this instruction is conditioned on the confidentiality requirements set out in this agreement. If the agreement never expires, as indicated in the changed text, there is a contradiction. The changed text creates a perpetual obligation of confidentiality, which directly contradicts the implicit assumption in the 'Return of Confidential Information' section that the obligation to keep the information confidential is tied to a defined period within the agreement.
